reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a Senior Salesforce Developer with 7+ years in CRM, Apex, Lightning, and DevOps. The JD demands an embedded C/AUTOSAR/RTOS engineer with RADAR and ADAS experience — a completely different engineering discipline. Not a single must-have skill from the JD appears anywhere in this resume. There is zero overlap between Salesforce cloud development and automotive embedded systems. Do not shortlist; move immediately to reject and source from automotive/embedded talent pools.
strengths: SALESFORCE ARCHITECTURE DEPTH: Led end-to-end delivery for a 1500+ user Salesforce org at IBM, building scalable Apex frameworks, LWC, and Flow-first architectures — strong signal for Salesforce roles, but entirely irrelevant here.

DEVOPS AND DEPLOYMENT MATURITY: Used Copado for CI/CD, achieved 90%+ test coverage across 500+ classes, and reduced deployment defects by 30% — impressive metrics, but zero transferability to embedded C/AUTOSAR context.

CERTIFICATIONS BREADTH: Holds 7 Salesforce certifications plus Copado and Azure credentials — demonstrates a credentialed professional, but none are relevant to automotive embedded systems or RADAR development.
gaps: NO EMBEDDED SYSTEMS EXPERIENCE WHATSOEVER: The role requires C programming, MISRA guidelines, AUTOSAR, and RTOS — core mandatory skills for any embedded automotive SW developer. This candidate has never worked in embedded or automotive domains.

ZERO RADAR/ADAS DOMAIN KNOWLEDGE: RADAR system knowledge and ADAS familiarity are explicit requirements. This candidate's entire career is in Salesforce CRM — there is no bridge to automotive sensing or perception engineering.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated in resume; no JD maximum specified, so no penalty applied.
Candidate location not explicitly stated in resume; cannot confirm Chennai mismatch with available data.
Candidate has 7+ years total experience, well above the minimum threshold of 2 years (half of 4 relevant required).
JD gender preference is 'Any'; no penalty applied.
No college exclusion or preference set in JD; no adjustment applied.

hard_filter_failures: Wrong domain — no embedded C, AUTOSAR, or RTOS experience; candidate fails role relevance threshold
availability_reason: Current role at IBM shows no end date and no notice period is stated in the resume.
alternative_role_fit: Senior Salesforce Developer or Salesforce Technical Lead
rare_assets: Salesforce Certified AI Specialist — a recently released, niche credential signalling early adoption of Salesforce Einstein/AI capabilities.
Copado Fundamentals I & II combined with hands-on CI/CD delivery — relatively rare combination of tooling certification and production DevOps execution in Salesforce ecosystem.
self_reported_unverified: 'Reduced deployment defects by 30%' — no baseline, measurement methodology, or external validation provided.
'Mentored 5 developers' — team size and actual mentoring outcomes beyond defect reduction are unverified.
'Production owner' claim at IBM — scope and authority of this role versus a formal production owner title is unclear from resume context.
interview_flags: Confirm whether candidate has any background in C programming or low-level systems — even academic projects from ECE degree.
Probe whether B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering included any embedded systems, RTOS, or signal processing coursework that could bridge to this role.
Ask directly: has candidate ever worked on automotive, ADAS, or any hardware-adjacent software project outside of Salesforce?
Verify the 'production owner' role at IBM — was this a formal designation or informal responsibility, and what did critical incident management involve?
Clarify career intent: is candidate actively looking to transition out of Salesforce into embedded/automotive, and if so, what retraining has been undertaken?
